title: orobanche cernua subsp. cumana
intro: Orobanche cernua subsp. cumana, also known as the broomrape or cuman broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanche genus and is native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Orobanche cernua subsp. cumana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for its medicinal and health benefits.
